plan.title: Integrate selected sub-agent backend into headless swarm launch and audit output

narratives.Description: This story makes the stored sub-agent backend policy load-bearing during headless swarm launch. It is independently buildable because it owns the launch-manifest consumption path and fail-loud checks, while depending on the policy/probe story for backend discovery and stored selection.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the source files scripts/swarm_launch.py and tasks/swarm.yml so task swarm:launch reads plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend, checks it against the current harness probe, and refuses headless dispatch when the policy is missing or unavailable.
2. Add focused tests in tests/cli/test_swarm_launch.py that verify missing-policy failure, unavailable-backend failure with listed alternatives, and successful launch-manifest audit fields for selected backend and worker role.
narratives.UserStory: As a headless swarm operator, I want task swarm:launch to enforce my selected sub-agent backend before dispatch, so that an unavailable Composer, Grok Build, or other agent never fails silently mid-swarm.

items.title: Given plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend is absent, when task swarm:launch is asked to emit a launch manifest for coding workers, then it exits non-zero before dispatch and tells the user to select a backend from the detected options.

narrative.Acceptance: Given plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend is absent, when task swarm:launch is asked to emit a launch manifest for coding workers, then it exits non-zero before dispatch and tells the user to select a backend from the detected options.

items.title: Given plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend names a backend that is unavailable in the current harness, when task swarm:launch runs, then it fails loud with the missing backend, the available backend IDs, and the command or prompt needed to choose a new backend.

narrative.Acceptance: Given plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend names a backend that is unavailable in the current harness, when task swarm:launch runs, then it fails loud with the missing backend, the available backend IDs, and the command or prompt needed to choose a new backend.

items.title: Given the selected backend is available, when task swarm:launch emits the launch manifest, then each entry includes audit-visible backend, dispatch provider, and worker role metadata without changing the existing allocation-context recognition contract.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the selected backend is available, when task swarm:launch emits the launch manifest, then each entry includes audit-visible backend, dispatch provider, and worker role metadata without changing the existing allocation-context recognition contract.

items.title: Given task swarm:launch runs with --autonomous, then missing or unavailable backend policy still fails non-interactively instead of prompting or falling back to an implicit default.

narrative.Acceptance: Given task swarm:launch runs with --autonomous, then missing or unavailable backend policy still fails non-interactively instead of prompting or falling back to an implicit default.

expected_outputs: swarm launch tests fail loud for missing or unavailable backend policy
launch manifest includes selected backend and worker-role audit metadata when backend is available

references.title: Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch (strong-model orchestrator + cheap high-context leaf code-runners)
Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch
